meta.purpose: Reasoning chains for why a motion decision is right or wrong.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks why, tradeoffs, diagnosis, quality review, or strategic choice.

reasoning_chains.conclusion: A motion design system must define token scales before building any primitives.
logic: Without tokens, every primitive decision is arbitrary and unreviewable.
Tokens create a shared language between designers and engineers.
When brand feel changes, only tokens need updating — not every component.
Tokens enable automated lint rules that prevent one-off animation drift.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Before writing any animation code in a team context, define: duration-sm, duration-md, duration-lg; ease-enter, ease-exit, ease-attention; and distance-near, distance-mid, distance-far.

reasoning_chains.conclusion: Motion primitives reduce long-term maintenance cost by 10x or more.
logic: Inline animation values in 40+ components create 40+ points of failure and inconsistency.
Changing the brand's motion feel requires touching every component individually.
A well-named primitive lets any team member add animations without deep motion knowledge.
Accessibility fixes (reduced-motion, vestibular safety) apply once in the primitive and propagate everywhere.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Any animation used in 3+ components must be extracted into a shared primitive. Treat inline animation values as technical debt.
